data.title: Shandong Fiberglass Group Change in Audit Personnel
data.description:
Shandong Fiberglass Group changed its audit personnel due to internal adjustments at Lixin Accounting Firm, according to a Thursday filing with the Shanghai Bourse.

Qu Yumin replaces Zhao Yong as the project quality control reviewer, while Zhu Mingjiang replaces Shi Huihui as the signing CPA.

The company ensures the transition will not impact its 2024 financial and internal control audits.

data.title: Hong Kong regulator in ‘active’ talks to introduce crypto ETF staking under new roadmap: HashKey
data.description:
Hong Kong may soon see the introduction of staking services for crypto exchange-traded funds and other advanced products, such as derivatives trading, following the release of a new industry roadmap. Major local crypto firms are actively in talks with the region's top financial regulator, The Block has learned.

HashKey, a major licensed crypto exchange in Hong Kong, is one of the many crypto players in frequent communication with the SFC. "We are actively discussing with the SFC the introduction of ETF staking and tokenized money market funds, as well as launching an 'Earn' feature alongside spot trading," Haiyang Ru, chief risk officer of HashKey Group, told The Block.

The Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission on Wednesday announced 12 initiatives, including exploring the possibility of new token listings, margin trading, derivatives, staking, borrowing and lending within the cryptocurrency industry.

Hong Kong has opened its door to the crypto industry. In June 2023, it officially launched a crypto licensing regime for “virtual asset trading platforms,” allowing licensed exchanges to offer retail trading services. However, with the goal of becoming a crypto hub, the regulator is now seeking to accelerate crypto development, especially amid intensifying competition due to the pro-crypto stance of Donald Trump, who recently took office in the U.S.

"Very soon, some of the world's top liquidity providers will land in Hong Kong. Stay tuned," said Ru, who declined to provide more details.

Ru also said that the consideration of staking would "enable HashKey Exchange to deliver secure and efficient staking services with support from HashKey Could."

Alessio Quaglini, co-founder and CEO of Hex Trust, told The Block that institutional staking is poised to be a major area of interest in 2025, "as institutions that move into crypto custody will naturally seek yield-generating opportunities for their clients." 

Expanding product offerings

OSL, another major licensed crypto exchange in Hong Kong, has also observed increasing customer interest in a broader range of services, including yield-generating products and derivatives, said Gary Tiu, executive director and head of regulatory affairs at OSL.

In terms of potential timeline, HashKey's Ru noted that the company hopes to introduce more advanced products — such as derivatives trading and margin financing — to professional investors in the second half of this year, provided that appropriate regulations are in place. "Once the market matures, these could gradually rollout to the retail," Ru said.

Ru also pointed out that Hong Kong faces fierce competition from other crypto-friendly jurisdictions, where regulatory frameworks are also evolving. "We expect at least three major initiatives could be fulfilled within 2025 [in Hong Kong]," he added.

Futu Securities, a brokerage in Hong Kong, also said that it's well-positioned to provide a wider range of choices for qualified retail investors once regulatory approval is granted. "We enthusiastically welcome the introduction of margin trading, derivatives, staking, and borrowing/lending services in crypto trading," Daniel Tse, managing director of Futu Securities International (Hong Kong) Ltd., told The Block.

Futu is a regulated brokerage firm that also offers BTC, ETH, LINK and AVAX to the retail market. "For investors whose risk tolerance assessment scores do not meet requirements for crypto assets, we offer alternative crypto investing opportunities such as crypto ETFs and crypto-concept stocks on our platform," Tse said.

Custodian licensing

As part of the roadmap, the SFC plans to establish licensing regimes for over-the-counter crypto trading and custodian services. “OTC desks, favored by institutions for conducting discreet large trades, operate with minimal transparency, thus heightening risks of market abuse,” the SFC wrote in the roadmap released Wednesday.

These frameworks are expected to establish a level playing field, "ensuring that all custodians meet rigorous anti-money laundering scrutiny and security, capital adequacy, and asset segregation requirements," said Quaglini of Hong Kong-headquartered Hex Trust, which offers institutional crypto custody, staking and markets services to builders and service providers.

"While retail investor protection remains a priority, what will position Hong Kong as a global leader is its efforts to develop a digital asset hub for institutional investors — spanning custody, liquidity, lending, borrowing and staking," said Quaglini. 

"We believe the roadmap has a very positive impact on the crypto industry in Hong Kong," said Ru of HashKey.

Disclaimer: The Block is an independent media outlet that delivers news, research, and data. As of November 2023, Foresight Ventures is a majority investor of The Block. Foresight Ventures invests in other companies in the crypto space. Crypto exchange Bitget is an anchor LP for Foresight Ventures. The Block continues to operate independently to deliver objective, impactful, and timely information about the crypto industry. Here are our current financial disclosures.

© 2025 The Block. All Rights Reserved. This article is provided for informational purposes only. It is not offered or intended to be used as legal, tax, investment, financial, or other advice.

data.title: European Equities Priced for Too Rosy a Macroeconomic Scenario — Market Talk
data.description: European equities are overpriced in the long run as investor optimism doesn't reflect the macroeconomic reality, BofA Global Research analysts write. European equities are pushing new all-time highs, with the Stoxx 600 up 12% from the post-U.S. election low in November, they write. However, just more than half of the rally is due to improving economic fundamentals, they add. The remainder of the gains are likely due to increased optimism on future European growth, fueled by a belief that there will be fiscal support in Germany, a potential Russia-Ukraine ceasefire, and increased European military spending, they write. However, the macro environment is set to remain weak and European equities are priced for too rosy a scenario, they write.(adam.whittaker@wsj.com)

data.title: UK Composite PMI Edges Lower
data.description:
The S&P Global UK Composite PMI was at 50.5 in February of 2025, inching lower from 50.6 in the previous month and in line with the market consensus to mark the 16th consecutive period of expansion in the British private sector activity.

Economic growth was solely driven by the services sector (51.1 vs 50.8 in January), which offset a sharper decline in manufacturing (46.4 vs 48.3), in line with the similar trend in other major European economies.

The latest survey indicated the sharpest contraction in new business received by firms in one-and-a-half years, with firms citing cuts to clients’ budgets and muted business investment spending.

data.title: JAFCO Group Invests in NEIVE to Enhance Automotive Inspection Jig Capabilities
data.description:
JAFCO Group has invested in NEIVE, a leading manufacturer of automotive inspection jigs, through its managed funds, according to its Friday filing.

NEIVE is known for its expertise and reliability in handling the entire production process of these jigs and is trusted by numerous automotive original equipment manufacturer (OEMs) and parts manufacturers.

As a strategic partner, JAFCO will support NEIVE by sharing management expertise and connecting it with advanced technologies and services from its portfolio companies.

data.title: UNION therapeutics announces presentation of preliminary data from Phase 2a investigator-initiated study with orismilast in ulcerative colitis at the 20th ECCO Congress
data.description:
UNION therapeutics
  • Poster presentation of preliminary data from the Phase 2a investigator-initiated study UCORIS in ulcerative colitis (UC) at the 20th ECCO Congress
  • UC is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease that causes inflammation and ulcers (sores) in the lower digestive tract with limited efficacious treatment options available
  • Orismilast is a high-potency PDE4B/D inhibitor with broad anti-inflammatory properties in development for oral treatment of inflammatory diseases, including hidradenitis suppurative (HS)

Hellerup, Denmark, February 21, 2025 – UNION therapeutics A/S (UNION), a privately-held, clinical stage, pharmaceutical development company focused on immunology, today announced a poster presentation on new clinical data of oral orismilast in patients with UC at the 20th European Crohn’s and Colitis Organisation (ECCO) congress on February 19-22, 2025, in Berlin, Germany.

UCORIS is a Phase 2a, open-label, investigator-initiated study investigating the efficacy and safety of oral orismilast for up to 12 weeks in adult patients with moderate and severe UC. Following the inclusion of 10 patients, the enrollment for the study has stopped with the study expected to be completed in 2025. The preliminary data from the proof-of-concept study suggests that orismilast exhibits therapeutic potential in moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis, with three patients achieving complete remission.

Poster details

Poster title: Orismilast for the Treatment of Moderate to Severe Ulcerative Colitis: A phase 2a, open-label, single-arm explorative clinical study

Authors: Trine Velte Honoré, Flemming Bendtsen, Jakob Seidelin, Johan Burisch

Kim Kjøller, Chief Executive Officer of UNION therapeutics said:

“We are encouraged by the investigators’ presentation of preliminary data on orismilast for the treatment of ulcerative colitis at the ECCO congress. UC is a systemic autoimmune bowel disease where a high unmet medical need persists for effective and safe treatments.”

 

About UC and treatment of UC

Ulcerative colitis (UC) is a chronic inflammatory bowel disease that causes inflammation and ulcers (sores) in the lower digestive tract, i.e. colon and rectum. More than 1.8 million patients are diagnosed with UC in the US, Japan and EU5 and the incidence of UC is increasing worldwide1). Despite the treatment options available today, an unmet medical need for safe oral treatments with improved efficacy remains. UC is a disease that can be in remission (less active) for longer periods of time and then suddenly flare (termed “relapse”). During periods of relapse, patients experience a significant impact on their everyday lives, experiencing symptoms such as bloody stool, diarrhea, fecal incontinence, increased frequency of bowel movements (urgency), fatigue, and abdominal pain (cramps).

Depending on the severity of UC and patient preferences, the treatment is tailored accordingly. Patients with mild UC are often treated with mild anti-inflammatory medication, taken orally or rectally, such as aminosalicylates, corticosteroids or immunomodulators. Patients with moderate to severe UC are often treated with immunosuppressants, biologics or a combination of such treatments. Overall, treatments aim to induce a rapid clinical response and maintain remission, ideally healing the ulcers and preventing long-term disability.

 

About orismilast

UNION is developing orismilast, a high-potency PDE4 inhibitor targeting the PDE4B/D subtypes linked to inflammation, demonstrating potent inhibition of Th1, Th2 and Th17 pathways. It operates early in the inflammation cascade, inducing a broad range of anti-inflammatory effects across multiple cytokines involved in many dermatological and immunological diseases.2)

Orismilast holds the potential to become a safe and efficacious oral treatment for several immunological diseases3), and UNION is currently developing oral orismilast for the treatment of hidradenitis suppurativa (HS).

The FDA has cleared UNION’s Investigational New Drug (IND) application for oral orismilast and granted Fast Track designation for oral orismilast for the treatment of moderate to severe HS as well as for the treatment of moderate to severe atopic dermatitis.

Sources

1)      EvaluatePharma (2024).

2)      Silverberg J.I. et al., Pharmacology of orismilast, a potent and selective PDE4 inhibitor, JEADV 2022: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jdv.18818  & Warren R.B. et al., Efficacy and safety in moderate-severe psoriasis and development of modified release tablets, JEADV 2022: https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1111/jdv.18812

3)      Warren R.B. et al., Orismilast in moderate-to-severe psoriasis: Efficacy and safety from a 16-week, randomized, double-blinded, placebo-controlled, dose-finding, and phase 2b trial (IASOS), J Am Acad Dermatol 2024: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37951245/ & Silverberg J.I. et al., Orismilast, a PDE4B/D inhibitor, in moderate-to-severe atopic dermatitis: Efficacy and safety from a multicenter, randomized, placebo-controlled, phase 2b dose-ranging study (ADESOS), Br J Dermatol 2025: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/39847538/. Frederiksen et al., JEADV 2023: https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/38147438/.

Contacts

Morten Boesen, Chief Financial Officer, UNION therapeutics A/S

+45 2381 5487

morten.boesen@uniontherapeutics.com

 

About UNION therapeutics

UNION therapeutics is a privately held, clinical stage, pharmaceutical development company focused on immunology. UNION is headquartered in Hellerup, Denmark, and led by an international team combining biotech entrepreneurs and seasoned pharma executives, with a track record of developing and launching multiple marketed drugs. Read more at www.uniontherapeutics.com

https://news.cision.com/union-therapeutics/r/union-therapeutics-announces-presentation-of-preliminary-data-from-phase-2a-investigator-initiated-s%2Cc4109415

data.title: Renault Must Seek to Maintain Growth From Its Solid Base — Market Talk
data.description: Renault is on a solid base and now needs to find a way to maintain EPS growth and cash returns, Citi analysts Harald C Hendrikse and Soumava Banerjee write. Renault's "Renaulution" transformation plan has been largely well executed, they say. The company has cut costs, cut losses and improved EBIT margins, and turned 3 billion net debt to over 7 billion net cash. Executing on the basics of sales discipline, attractive consumer products and low costs has worked well. "With over 20 euros per share in cash, over 20 euros per share in Mobilize equity, and around 12 euros per share in Nissan value, investors still pay zero for the core franchise," they say. Shares rise 0.7% to 49.46 euros. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Luxus Home & Garden Introduces Sustainable Outdoor Living Trends for Spring 2025
data.description:
London, United Kingdom--(Newsfile Corp. - February 21, 2025) - As the demand for eco-friendly and stylish outdoor spaces continues to grow, Luxus Home & Garden is unveiling the top sustainable outdoor living trends for Spring 2025. With a focus on environmentally responsible materials, durable designs, and multifunctional furniture, the brand is helping homeowners transform their gardens into eco-conscious retreats.

With spring just around the corner, now is the perfect time for homeowners to refresh their outdoor spaces. From ethically sourced garden furniture to weather-resistant garden sofa sets and sustainable garden benches, Luxus Home & Garden is leading the charge in providing luxurious yet responsible outdoor solutions.

Sustainability Meets Style: The Key Outdoor Living Trends for 2025

  • Eco-Friendly Materials Take Center Stage

    Consumers are becoming increasingly conscious of their environmental footprint. Luxus Home & Garden is responding by offering sustainably sourced teak and recyclable materials in its garden furniture collections. These pieces are designed to last for decades, reducing waste and promoting responsible consumption.

  • Modular and Multifunctional Designs

    The trend toward flexible outdoor spaces is growing, with homeowners seeking adaptable furniture that fits multiple uses. The garden sofa sets from Luxus Home & Garden are designed with versatility in mind, allowing users to reconfigure seating arrangements to suit different occasions.

  • Biophilic Design for a Natural Feel

    Bringing nature closer to home remains a top priority for many garden enthusiasts. Expect to see an emphasis on organic shapes, natural textures, and plant-friendly furniture designs that seamlessly blend with the surrounding environment. The garden benches from Luxus Home & Garden complement this trend by offering timeless, nature-inspired seating options.

  • Durable, Weather-Resistant Furniture for All Seasons

    With unpredictable weather patterns, outdoor furniture must be built to withstand the elements. Luxus Home & Garden's premium collections feature high-quality teak, rust-resistant metals, and UV-resistant fabrics, ensuring that garden spaces stay beautiful year-round.

Elevating Outdoor Living with Luxus Home & Garden

"At Luxus Home & Garden, we believe that sustainability should never come at the expense of style or comfort," said Sumit Bhardwaj, Marketing Consultant at Luxus Home & Garden. "Our goal for Spring 2025 is to offer furniture that not only enhances outdoor spaces but also aligns with our commitment to eco-friendly living."

As the spring season approaches, homeowners looking to refresh their gardens can explore a variety of options at Luxus Home & Garden's online store. Whether it's a statement teak garden sofa set or a classic wooden garden bench, the brand's collections reflect both modern innovation and timeless craftsmanship.

Discover Sustainable Garden Furniture for 2025

For more information on sustainable outdoor furniture and to explore the latest trends, visit:

  • Garden furniture
  • Garden sofa sets
  • Garden benches

For press inquiries or to schedule an interview, contact:

Sumit Bhardwaj

Marketing Consultant

Luxus Home & Garden

71-75 Shelton Street

Covent Garden, London

WC2H 9JQ, United Kingdom

Phone: 0333 772 9013

Email: info@luxushomeandgarden.com

To view the source version of this press release, please visit https://www.newsfilecorp.com/release/241758

data.title: Aurora Cannabis Expands Product Portfolio in Australia
data.description:
Aurora Cannabis said late Thursday it has expanded its medical cannabis pastille offerings in Australia.

The product lineup in Australia now include three variants containing tetrahydrocannabinol and/or cannabidiol.

data.title: Street View: Brokerages bullish on Walmart despite weak FY outlook
data.description:
** Walmart on Thursday forecast sales for fiscal year ending January 2026 below Wall-Street estimates, signaling world's largest retailer expects inflation-weary consumers to pull back after several quarters of solid growth

** Average rating of 43 brokerages covering WMT is "buy"; PT: $110 - data compiled by LSEG

MARKET MASTERY A BUYERS' DREAM

** J.P.Morgan ("overweight," PT: $112) is optimistic about WMT, highlighting strong growth in traditional retail drivers and strategic reinvestments

** RBC Capital Markets ("outperform," PT: $107) believes WMT's fundamentals remain strong despite disappointing FY26 guidance

** Jefferies ("buy," PT: $120) views WMT's FY guidance as conservative, expecting co to exceed its targets with accelerating profit growth compared to the last two years

** Bernstein ("outperform") views WMT's strong market share potential and e-commerce improvements as a buying opportunity despite conservative guidance

data.title: Bulgarian Rose Jan sales slightly above estimate
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), February 21 (SeeNews) - Cosmetics producer Bulgarian Rose [BUL:ROZA] said its sales revenue amounted to 408,000 levs ($217,849/208,606 euro) in January, above its 386,000 levs forecast.

Last month, Bulgarian Rose posted a preliminary loss of 16,000 levs, the company said in a bourse filing on Thursday. To compare, the company booked sales of 264,000 levs and a loss of 28,000 levs in January 2024.

Sales in February are projected at 334,000 levs, bringing revenue for the first two months of 2025 to 742,000 levs.

Karlovo-based Bulgarian Rose is part of local diversified group Stara Planina Hold [BUL:SPH]. Shares in the company last traded on February 17 when they closed at 1.90 levs on the BaSE market of the Bulgarian Stock Exchange, bourse data show.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: UK Manufacturing Sector Contracts the Most in 14 Months
data.description:
The S&P Global UK Manufacturing PMI dropped to 46.4 in February 2025 from 48.3 in the previous month and below market expectations of 48.4, a preliminary estimate showed.

The latest reading pointed to the weakest pace of expansion in the sector since December 2023, as output decreased for the fourth month in a row and at a faster pace, and sales fell in both domestic and overseas markets.

In addition, both employment levels and unfinished business dropped sharply.

On the price front, input cost inflation accelerated amid greater raw material costs and energy bills, while factory gate prices rose the most since April 2023.

data.title: BioStock: Medivir's investigator on the positive results directly from EASL
data.description:
Medivir

This week, Medivir presented positive final results from the phase Ib/IIa study on the combination of Fostrox and Lenvima for the treatment of advanced liver cancer. The results, presented at the EASL Liver Cancer Summit, demonstrate promising efficacy and safety. We spoke with Dr. Jeff Evans, one of the study investigators, directly from EASL in Paris.

data.title: UK Services Activity Expands Slightly Faster
data.description:
The S&P Global UK Services PMI rose to 51.1 in February 2025, up from January's 50.8 and beating market forecasts of 50.8, preliminary data showed.

The latest data showed that the sector growth gained some momentum in February, but the overall pace of output expansion was much softer than the long-run survey average.

Anecdotal evidence pointed to fewer new projects and cautious client spending due to UK economic concerns, with global uncertainty also limiting February's growth.

data.title: Leonardo's U.S. Division DRS Should Report Rising Sales in 2025 — Market Talk
data.description: Leonardo DRS, a U.S. subsidiary of Italian aerospace and defense group Leonardo, should continue to report increasing sales this year, JP Morgan analysts say. The company significantly beat its top-line expectations in 2024, with international sales growing much faster than U.S. sales, they say. Most of the company's international sales relate to sensing and computing equipment to help armed forces make quicker decisions on the battlefield, but over time DRS could receive a higher number of orders for counter drones and short-range air defense systems, the analysts say. The company ended 2024 with about $600 million of cash, implying it could continue to return at least $135 million to shareholders each year, a level described by the analysts as quite reasonable. DRS could also continue pursuing mergers and acquisition deals, they add. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Sterling Edges Down Slightly
data.description:
The British pound edged down to $1.265 after early February PMI data signaled stalled business activity for the fourth straight month, with job losses rising amid weaker sales and higher costs.

This stagnation, combined with persistent inflation, raises concerns about a stagflationary environment, posing a challenge for the Bank of England.

For the week, the sterling is up around 0.5%, and hit the highest level since December 17 after a hotter-than-expected inflation print.

Meanwhile, UK retail sales exceeded all expectations, including and excluding fuel.

The ONS also reported a UK budget surplus of £15.4 billion in January, falling short of the £20.3 billion estimate.

GfK’s consumer confidence index remained negative, though all key metrics improved in February.

data.title: Leonardo's 2024 Results Are Positive Across the Board — Market Talk
data.description: Leonardo's 2024 results are to be considered positive, especially the free cash flow, which has always been the group's weak point, Equita analyst Martino De Ambroggi says. Free operating cash flow stood at 826 million euros last year, up 26.7% on 2023. However, the results don't include MBDA's contribution to Ebita, factoring, capitalized research and innovation, or the real cash burn of the aerostructures division, De Ambroggi says. MBDA is a joint venture formed by Airbus, BAE Systems and Leonardo to manufacture missiles. Many important messages have been postponed to the business plan update on March 11, including continuing talks with Airbus and Thales to form a space joint venture, he says. Such plans will be based on the current level of NATO's defense spending, which is expected to rise, so the plan might be considered a floor, De Ambroggi says. Shares in the Italian aerospace and defense manufacturer are up 1.9% at 35.47 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Eniro Group AB appoints Stefan Liljedahl as Interim CFO
data.description:
Eniro Group

Eniro Group AB announced today that Stefan Liljedahl has been appointed as the new Interim Chief Financial Officer (CFO) during the ongoing recruitment process for a permanent CFO. Stefan will start his new role on March 10, 2025.

Stefan Liljedahl has an extensive background in senior financial positions in both listed and private environments. Most recently, Stefan served as Interim CFO & Head of IT/Supply Chain at Indiska.

- We are very pleased to welcome Stefan as Interim CFO for Eniro. With over 20 years of experience in senior roles at global companies, including as Interim Group Head of FP&A at Sinch Group, he will bring strong expertise in financial management, M&A, and business development, says Hosni Teque-Omeirat, CEO of Eniro.

For more information, please contact:

Hosni Teque-Omeirat, president and CEO Eniro Group AB (publ)

Tel: +46 (0)70-225 18 77

E-post: hosni.teque-omeirat@eniro.com

This information is information that Eniro Group AB (publ) is obliged to make public pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation. The information was submitted for publication, through the agency of the contact person set out above, at 10:40CET on 21 February 2025. 

Eniro exists for companies that want to achieve success and growth in their market. Today, Eniro optimizes the opportunity for companies to create local presence, searchability and marketing digitally. This makes Eniro an important partner for small and medium-sized companies. The company's clear goal is to give SMEs the same conditions and resources that large companies have access to. Eniro offers a platform that optimizes local marketing through intelligence, automation and streamlining of communication. In the digital landscape, Eniro partners with the largest media groups in the world.

Eniro Group AB (publ) is listed on Nasdaq Stockholm (ENRO) and operates in Sweden, Denmark, Finland and Norway. In 2023, the Eniro Group had sales of SEK 960 million and approximately 900 employees with headquarters in Stockholm. The group also includes Dynava, which offers customer service and answering services for major companies in the Nordic region, as well as directory assistance services.

Stefan 2

data.title: Thai rice farmers demand better support as prices plummet
data.description:
Thai government measures to support rice farmers have failed to meet expectations, with some growers in the world's second-largest rice exporting country vowing protests as they are squeezed between falling prices and rising cultivation costs.

Paddy prices have dropped by 30% year-on-year to an average of about 8,600 baht ($256) per ton this month, the commerce ministry said, piling pressure on the Pheu Thai party-led government to placate rice farmers - a key vote bank.

"We are not happy with the measures. I think the government is not sincere with farmers at all," said Thitiwat Kleepmalai, a farmer leader from Ayutthaya province who had submitted his group's demands to the government on Wednesday.

The government on Thursday announced measures worth 1.89 billion baht, including providing loans for farmers to delay paddy sales, assisting with storage fees, and covering interest costs for rice mills to store the crop.

These measures, which are yet to be approved by the cabinet, are part of an effort by the government to keep paddy prices above 8,000 baht per ton, Commerce Minister Pichai Naripthaphan told reporters.

The Ayutthaya group had asked the government to either help them sell rice at 11,000 baht per ton, or provide a guaranteed price at the same level, as production costs are as high as 6,500 baht per ton, Thitiwat said.

Thailand's centuries-old rice cultivation system is under severe stress from climate change, unsustainable farm debts and a lack of innovation, despite tens of billions of dollars in subsidies over the past decade.

Thitiwat said his group will meet with other provincial farm leaders to organise a larger protest in Bangkok this month, following an earlier rally.

Pui Saengnak, another farmer from Ayutthaya, said if there are no further measures, he and other rice growers will take to the streets in large numbers.

"Farmers are suffering greatly," he said.

The resumption of rice exports by India - the world's largest shipper of the grain - is likely to hit Thailand hard, with the commerce ministry expecting a 24% year-on-year decline in rice exports to 7.5 million tons this year.

That will follow a 13.4% rise in rice shipments to 9.95 million tons last year, the highest level in six years, according to the commerce ministry.

($1 = 33.60 baht)

data.title: Celsius surges on deal to buy wellness drinks brand Alani Nutrition
data.description:
** Shares of energy drink maker Celsius Holdings surge 33.6% premarket

** Celsius to buy health and wellness drinks brand Alani Nutrition in $1.8 billion deal

** Wedbush analyst Gerald Pascarelli says, "This deal makes good sense as Alani is one of the fastest growing brands in energy with meaningful distribution whitespace"

** Celsius also posts better-than-expected Q4 revenue and adjusted EPS

** Up to last close, stock had fallen 3% YTD; it slid 52% in 2024

data.title: Philips Partners Mass General Brigham to launch AI-Driven Healthcare Data Platform
data.description:
Royal Philips said Friday it partnered with US-based Mass General Brigham to develop and process an advanced AI-powered data infrastructure for real-time clinical decision-making.

The collaboration will integrate the Dutch conglomerate's medical device data streaming technology with electronic medical records and lab results for an actionable patient data environment.

The partnership will initially focus on real-time heart monitoring for early cardiac event detection and intervention by leveraging AI to generate smart alerts.

data.title: COMMENT-Central bank policies may have very negative consequences
data.description:
Central banks easing policy to boost their economies are at the same time stoking inflation that they are struggling to control, a paradox which may have very negative consequences for financial markets.

Since 2008 the remedy for almost every problem confronting policymakers has been to spend their way out of trouble. But not only has that boosted stocks to record levels, it has supported inflation that remains above target in most major economies. Yet central banks are easing policy, or hoping to do so.

Stocks have done what stocks always seem to do, rising further, with the MSCI world equity index setting a record high this month. But there has also been a big rise in gas prices that suggest inflation will rise.

Should OPEC delay its plan to increase supply in April, the chance of a more sustainable decline in prices may evaporate, and central banks may be forced not only to abandon plans to cut interest rates, but also to consider raising them.

Stocks and other risky assets like Bitcoin that are at the peak of extremely big rises are apt to reverse. Even a minor reverse of their previous gains would appear to be an extremely risk averse event for traders who are mainly looking in one direction.

In such circumstances the dollar could rocket, suppressing inflation in the United States and boosting it elsewhere, forcing interest rates higher, further undermining stocks.

Investors focused on euro may find it easier to hedge.

data.title: Hwa Tai Industries Names COO
data.description: Hwa Tai Industries appointed Teo Giap Cheng as its chief operating officer on Thursday, according to a same-day Malaysian bourse filing.

data.title: Webstep Extends Frame Agreement with Equinor
data.description:
Webstep struck a frame agreement to support Equinor's digitalization efforts for three years, with an option for an extra year.

The deal extends the framework agreement signed between the Norwegian IT and consulting company and the energy giant in 2021, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Poolbeg Pharma Says Hookipa Ended Takeover Talks
data.description:
Poolbeg Pharma said Friday that US-based Hookipa Pharma terminated the discussions on the proposed combination of the two companies.

Hookipa had until Feb. 27 to confirm or end the nonbinding talks for an all-share acquisition of the British biopharmaceutical company. The negotiations started on Jan. 2.
